implemented_capabilities.summary: Ensure Animator exists on entity with optional sheet (creates or updates)

example.description: Ensure Player has Animator with sprite sheet
example.expected_outcome: Animator exists on Player with sprite_sheet set to assets/player.png (created if missing, sheet updated if different)

implemented_capabilities.notes: Idempotent operation. If Animator does NOT exist: creates it with the provided sheet. If Animator ALREADY exists and no sheet provided: succeeds without changes. If Animator ALREADY exists and sheet provided: updates the sheet. This provides a single-command 'ensure exists with this configuration' workflow ideal for headless automation.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Get detailed information about an entity's Animator

example.expected_outcome: Returns sprite_sheet, frame size, states list with durations

implemented_capabilities.notes: Useful for debugging animation states and verifying configuration.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Set the sprite sheet asset for an Animator

example.expected_outcome: Player's Animator now references the specified sprite sheet

implemented_capabilities.notes: The asset must have slices defined. Used before creating animation states. Requires Animator to already exist; use 'animator ensure' if you need to create and set sheet in one operation.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Create or update an animation state

example.description: Create idle animation state using slice_0 through slice_3
example.expected_outcome: 'idle' state created on Player's Animator (upserts if exists)

implemented_capabilities.notes: Upserts: creates if not exists, updates if exists. Use --auto-create to create Animator component if missing. First state becomes default. --loop enables looping (default), --no-loop disables it.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Remove an animation state from an Animator

example.expected_outcome: 'hurt' state removed. Default and current state updated if they referenced 'hurt'. Any on_complete references to 'hurt' are cleared (set to null).

implemented_capabilities.notes: CAN remove the last state (schema allows empty animations object). When the last state is removed, default_state is set to the removed state's name as a placeholder (schema requires non-empty string). When removing a state, on_complete references pointing to it from other states are automatically cleared.

implemented_capabilities.summary: List all assets in the project catalog

example.expected_outcome: Returns filtered list of asset records with paths and guids

implemented_capabilities.notes: Search matches against asset paths. Results include guid, path, asset_kind, importer.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Auto-detect slices from a sprite sheet asset

example.expected_outcome: Slices auto-detected and saved to asset metadata

implemented_capabilities.notes: Uses alpha channel to detect sprite boundaries. Preview mode shows detections without saving.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Create grid-based slices from a sprite sheet asset

example.description: Slice a 256x256 sprite sheet into 32x32 tiles
example.expected_outcome: Asset metadata updated with grid-based slice definitions

implemented_capabilities.notes: Creates uniform slices based on cell size. Non-uniform regions require manual slicing.

implemented_capabilities.summary: List all slices defined for an asset

example.description: List slices for player sprite sheet
example.expected_outcome: Returns list of slice definitions with names and rectangles

implemented_capabilities.notes: Slices are stored in asset metadata and referenced by name in Animator.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Save manually defined slices for an asset

example.expected_outcome: Manual slices saved to asset metadata

implemented_capabilities.notes: Use for non-uniform sprites that don't fit grid or auto-detection.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Add a component to an existing entity

example.description: Add a Sprite to the Player
example.expected_outcome: Player entity now has a Sprite component

implemented_capabilities.notes: Component data is optional; defaults are used if not provided. Fails if component already exists. Use canonical component names from the component registry.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Create a new entity with optional components

example.description: Create a player entity with Transform
example.expected_outcome: Entity 'Player' exists in active scene with Transform component

implemented_capabilities.notes: Entity names must be unique in the scene. Components are optional at creation. Use --components '{"Transform":{"x":100}}' to add components.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Query this capability registry itself

example.description: List all capabilities in JSON format
example.expected_outcome: Returns full capability registry as JSON

implemented_capabilities.notes: The registry is accessible via 'motor capabilities' CLI command. Use for discovering available operations.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Diagnose project health and detect issues

example.expected_outcome: Returns diagnostic report with checks, warnings, and recommendations

implemented_capabilities.notes: Read-only operation. Validates project.json, motor_ai.json, START_HERE_AI.md, and directory structure.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Generate AI bootstrap files (motor_ai.json and START_HERE_AI.md)

example.description: Generate AI bootstrap files for the project
example.expected_outcome: motor_ai.json and START_HERE_AI.md are created in project root

implemented_capabilities.notes: Regenerates the AI-facing documentation files. Safe to run multiple times (idempotent).

implemented_capabilities.summary: Get the current project's manifest summary

example.expected_outcome: Returns project name, root, paths, engine_version

implemented_capabilities.notes: Read-only. Use for discovering project structure programmatically.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Create a new scene with a unique file path

example.expected_outcome: A new scene file is created at levels/level_1.json and becomes the active scene

implemented_capabilities.notes: Scene name is sanitized to snake_case for the filename. Avoids collisions automatically.

implemented_capabilities.summary: List all available scenes in the project

example.description: List all scenes in the project
example.expected_outcome: Returns list of scene paths and names

implemented_capabilities.notes: Searches levels/ directory for .json scene files.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Load a scene from a JSON file path

example.expected_outcome: The scene is loaded and becomes the active world

implemented_capabilities.notes: Supports relative paths from project root. Creates world from serialized scene data.

implemented_capabilities.summary: Save the active scene to its source file

example.expected_outcome: The active scene is serialized and written to its source_path

implemented_capabilities.notes: Saves the currently active scene. Requires a scene to be loaded first.

planned_capabilities.summary: Find assets by kind, importer, or extension

example.expected_outcome: Returns list of texture assets with metadata

planned_capabilities.notes: Multiple filters can be combined. Empty result means no matches.

planned_capabilities.summary: Get metadata for a specific asset

example.expected_outcome: Returns asset metadata including guid, importer settings, dependencies

planned_capabilities.notes: Metadata includes slices if the asset has been sliced.

planned_capabilities.summary: Refresh the asset catalog and detect changes

example.expected_outcome: Asset catalog is updated with any new or modified assets

planned_capabilities.notes: Run this after adding new files to assets/ folder.

planned_capabilities.summary: Edit a property of an existing component

example.expected_outcome: Player's Transform.x is updated to 200

planned_capabilities.notes: Property names are component-specific. Type conversion is automatic.

planned_capabilities.summary: Remove a component from an entity

example.description: Remove the Collider from an entity
example.expected_outcome: Collider component is removed from Player

planned_capabilities.notes: Some components (like Transform) are required and cannot be removed. Use canonical component names from the component registry.

planned_capabilities.summary: Remove an entity from the active scene, reparenting children to grandparent

example.expected_outcome: Entity 'Player' is removed; any children are reparented to Player's parent (or unparented if Player had no parent). Children preserve their world transform.

planned_capabilities.notes: CHILDREN ARE NOT DELETED - they are reparented to the deleted entity's parent (grandparent). Their local transforms are recalculated to preserve world position. Use remove_entity_subtree for recursive deletion. Cannot be undone automatically.

planned_capabilities.summary: List all entities in the active scene, optionally filtered

example.description: List all active entities with the 'Enemy' tag
example.expected_outcome: Returns list of EntityData for matching entities

planned_capabilities.notes: Filtering is optional. Returns empty list if no entities match.

planned_capabilities.summary: Set or change an entity's parent for hierarchical transforms

example.description: Parent a weapon to the player
example.expected_outcome: Sword becomes a child of Player and inherits its transform

planned_capabilities.notes: Pass None/null as parent to unparent. Child transform is applied relative to parent.

planned_capabilities.summary: Get full data for a specific entity

example.expected_outcome: Returns EntityData with all components and values

planned_capabilities.notes: Throws EntityNotFoundError if entity doesn't exist.

planned_capabilities.summary: Get engine status including FPS, entity count, time

example.expected_outcome: Returns state, frame, time, fps, entity_count

planned_capabilities.notes: Lightweight status check. Safe to call frequently.

planned_capabilities.summary: List available physics backends and their status

example.expected_outcome: Returns list of backends with availability status

planned_capabilities.notes: Backends include 'legacy_aabb' (always available) and optional 'box2d'.

planned_capabilities.summary: Query physics entities within an axis-aligned bounding box

example.expected_outcome: Returns list of entities with colliders in the region

planned_capabilities.notes: Uses active physics backend. Results include entity names and collision data.

planned_capabilities.summary: Cast a ray and find intersecting physics bodies

example.description: Cast ray from player position looking right
example.expected_outcome: Returns hit information sorted by distance

planned_capabilities.notes: Direction is normalized. Returns empty list if no hits.

planned_capabilities.summary: Apply instance overrides back to the source prefab

example.description: Apply Enemy_01 changes to the prefab
example.expected_outcome: Prefab file is updated with the instance's overrides

planned_capabilities.notes: Only works on prefab instances. Updates the source .prefab file.

planned_capabilities.summary: Create an entity instance from a prefab file

example.expected_outcome: Entity 'Enemy_01' created with all prefab components

planned_capabilities.notes: Creates a linked instance. Changes to prefab can be propagated to instances.

planned_capabilities.summary: List all prefabs available in the project

example.expected_outcome: Returns list of prefab paths

planned_capabilities.notes: Searches prefabs/ directory and lists .prefab and .json files.

planned_capabilities.summary: Convert a prefab instance to a regular entity

example.description: Unpack Enemy_01 to modify it independently
example.expected_outcome: Entity is no longer linked to prefab, becomes editable

planned_capabilities.notes: Breaks the prefab link. Entity keeps its components but won't receive prefab updates.

planned_capabilities.summary: Get or set editor state including recent assets and last scene

example.expected_outcome: Returns recent_assets, last_scene, open_scenes, preferences

planned_capabilities.notes: Editor state is persisted in .motor/editor_state.json

planned_capabilities.summary: Open a different project and load its startup scene

example.expected_outcome: Project is opened and last scene (or first available) is loaded

planned_capabilities.notes: Switches the active project context. Unsaved changes in current project may be lost.

planned_capabilities.summary: Start play mode to test game logic

example.expected_outcome: Engine enters PLAY mode, scripts with run_in_edit_mode execute

planned_capabilities.notes: Only available in EDIT mode. Scripts can control entities during play.

planned_capabilities.summary: Redo a previously undone operation

example.expected_outcome: Previously undone change is reapplied

planned_capabilities.notes: Only available if undo was called. Cleared on new edits.

planned_capabilities.summary: Advance the simulation by N frames

example.expected_outcome: World updates for 10 frames

planned_capabilities.notes: Useful for debugging. Can be called during play or step-through mode.

planned_capabilities.summary: Stop play mode and return to edit mode

example.expected_outcome: Engine returns to EDIT mode, world state is restored

planned_capabilities.notes: Restores world to pre-play state. Autosave may preserve changes.

planned_capabilities.summary: Undo the last edit operation

example.expected_outcome: Last change is reverted

planned_capabilities.notes: Uses scene manager's change tracking. Not all operations are undoable.

planned_capabilities.summary: Load the configured next scene in the scene flow

example.description: Transition to the next configured scene
example.expected_outcome: The next_scene from current scene metadata is loaded

planned_capabilities.notes: Uses next_scene key from feature_metadata.scene_flow. Fails if not configured.

planned_capabilities.summary: Set the next scene connection for scene flow navigation

example.description: Configure current scene to load level2 when triggered
example.expected_outcome: Scene metadata updated with next_scene reference

planned_capabilities.notes: Stores relative path in scene metadata. Used by load_next_scene at runtime.
